seoTitle: How to Add Expense Categories in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Create new expense categories and sub-accounts in your QuickBooks Online Chart of Accounts to keep your books organized.
intro: As your business grows, your default expense categories may not cover every type of cost you're tracking. This guide covers creating a new expense account in your Chart of Accounts, choosing the right detail type, and nesting it as a sub-account if needed. It's for business owners customizing their categories ahead of tax season or expanded reporting needs.

steps.heading: Open the Chart of Accounts
steps.body: Go to Transactions, then select Chart of Accounts to reach your full account directory.

steps.heading: Start a new account
steps.body: Click New in the upper right corner to open the account configuration panel.

steps.heading: Choose account and detail type
steps.body: Select Expenses as the Account Type, then pick the specific Detail Type that best matches the tax classification for this category.

steps.heading: Name and nest the category
steps.body: Type a clear name for the category, and check Make This a Sub-Account if it should sit under a broader parent expense line.

steps.heading: Add a description and save
steps.body: Include an internal note describing what belongs in this category, then click Save to add it to your chart.

faq.question: What's the difference between Account Type and Detail Type?
faq.answer: Account Type is the broad classification like Expenses, while Detail Type narrows it down to a specific tax-line category within that group.

faq.question: Can I turn an existing category into a sub-account later?
faq.answer: Yes, you can edit an existing account and check the sub-account box to nest it under a parent category.

faq.question: Will adding a new category affect past transactions?
faq.answer: No, new categories only apply going forward; past transactions stay coded to whatever category was originally used.

faq.question: How do I make sure my new category shows up on forms?
faq.answer: Test it by creating a new expense or checking your bank feed rules to confirm the fresh category appears in the dropdown lists.
